summary: Iter-43 backend+frontend testing. Feature 1 (Firm Master Business Type dropdown) and Feature 2 (per-firm Attendance Policy): 16/16 backend pytests PASS (test_iter43_business_type_and_policy.py). Frontend happy-paths verified: /company-register bottom-sheet picker (Industry → Textile) works, /companies 'Add company' with Hotel / Resort creates a card with the correct badge, /attendance-policy renders all sections, weekday toggle + save + Presets sheet all work when a valid company_id is passed. ONE frontend bug: super-admin's 'Attendance policy' tile on /admin sends '?company_id=all' when the filter is on the default 'All companies', causing the AP screen to spin forever (backend responds 404).

ui_bugs.issue: When super_admin has the company filter set to 'All companies' (default), tapping the 'Attendance policy' tile navigates to /attendance-policy?company_id=all. Backend responds 404 (no company matches 'all'), and the AP screen shows an endless spinner. Fix: change the condition on line 205 to `isSuper && companyFilter && companyFilter !== 'all'` (or omit the param entirely when 'all') AND update /attendance-policy.tsx to prompt the super admin to pick a company when no company_id is passed instead of hanging on a spinner.

action_items: Fix admin.tsx 'open-attendance-policy' onPress: only pass company_id when it is a real company id (not the sentinel 'all'). Also add a graceful 'Please pick a company first' UI in attendance-policy.tsx when super_admin lands without company_id (currently loads forever on 404).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L2980 — is_default_preset = company.get('attendance_policy') is None. Since POST /companies always auto-attaches a preset via _policy_for_category, is_default_preset will be false for every newly-created company even if the admin has never customized. Consider distinguishing 'auto-preset attached' from 'user-customized' by checking attendance_policy_updated_at instead (spec says 'If the company has no override yet, is_default_preset = true').
seed_data_creation: Test companies prefixed TEST_ are created and cleaned up by pytest teardown. One UI-created 'TEST_HotelUI_<hex>' Hotel/Resort company remains in the DB from the manual frontend flow — safe to leave or delete via /companies.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend uses OTP dev-mode; /api/auth/otp/request returns dev_code so no email round-trip. Public preview URL is EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL in /app/frontend/.env. To provision a company_admin session without touching super_admin PIN, POST /api/companies with admin_phone and admin_name — response returns temp_pin, then hit /api/auth/admin-pin-login with {identifier: phone, pin: temp_pin}. When adding UI tests for /attendance-policy as super_admin, ALWAYS pass a real company_id (do not rely on the admin tile until the 'all' bug is fixed).
rca_of_issue: Bug: super_admin /admin → 'Attendance policy' tile → 404 loop. Root cause: admin.tsx defaults companyFilter to the string 'all' (line 27) but the tile's condition (line 205) is `isSuper && companyFilter`, which is truthy for 'all'. It pushes /attendance-policy?company_id=all. The backend GET /api/attendance/policy then runs `db.companies.find_one({company_id: 'all'})`, gets no match, and returns 404. The frontend AP screen stays in the loading state because its fetch fails and there is no error UI branch for that path. Repro: login as super_admin sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com → /admin → without changing the 'All companies' filter, tap Attendance policy tile → observe endless spinner and console 404. Fix (small): (a) admin.tsx onPress → use `companyFilter && companyFilter !== 'all' ? params : no params`; (b) attendance-policy.tsx → if role==super_admin and no company_id, show inline picker or friendly 'Select a company from Admin → Filter first' message and stop the spinner.
